title: Graduate School of Sciences Research and Development
content:
Graduate School of Sciences Research and Development

Research and Development

Studies and Goals within the Scope AR-GE (1/2)

Our Graduate School's research strategies and objectives are in line with our University's 2019-2023 Strategic plan.

Research Strategy of Our Graduate School:

-To increase R&D resources and effectiveness, -To strengthen the culture of science, research, creativity and innovation, -To support the working and research process with a high motivation of the faculty members and students within the Graduate School, -To use technology and technology by producing new information with researches made by considering national priorities. Contributing to universal science-Producing and disseminating scientific knowledge and making it available to the public,-Encouraging academia and stakeholders to contribute to the national economy by transforming scientific knowledge into technology,-Ensuring the effective use of Research and Application Centers in postgraduate thesis and project studies, and the centers and their duties -To inform our students about international collaborations and academic partnerships, to encourage academic collaborations.

Studies and Goals within the scope of AR-GE (1/2)

In line with the 2024-2028 Strategic Plan of our University;

PARGE

(Project, Research and Development)

, laboratories,

Application and Research Centers

,

and scientific working groups create physical infrastructure and financial resources for research and development activities.

Research activities at our university are monitored through the Activity Tracking System (ATS). The determination, implementation, control and measures of policies and targets for performance improvement are in the planning stage. The 2023-2024 research performance of our faculty is kept as an activity table and is also monitored through the ATS system.

In our university, the Scientific Incentive Directive is also implemented in order to develop the competencies of the faculty members.
